journal_entry.description: Deposit received for moving services to be performed in one month
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS 15, revenue is recognized when performance obligations are satisfied. Since the moving services will be performed in one month, the $5,000 deposit received creates a liability (unearned revenue/deferred revenue) until the services are performed. The bank account (asset) increases by the deposit amount. This is a straightforward application of the revenue recognition principle - cash received in advance of service delivery creates a contract liability.

output.reason: Atlas receives $5,000 cash deposit via bank transfer. This increases the chequing account (bank/cash asset). Debit: 1 asset increase. The credit side would record unearned revenue (liability increase) since the moving services have not yet been performed.

debit_classifier.reason: Atlas receives $5,000 cash deposit via bank transfer. This increases the chequing account (bank/cash asset). Debit: 1 asset increase. The credit side would record unearned revenue (liability increase) since the moving services have not yet been performed.

output.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit, Atlas has two credit-side effects: (1) Cash/chequing account increases = asset increase, but we classify CREDITS only, so this is on the debit side. (2) The deposit creates an obligation to perform services = unearned revenue/deferred revenue, which is a liability increase. Therefore, the credit side has one line: liability increase for the unearned revenue.

credit_classifier.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit, Atlas has two credit-side effects: (1) Cash/chequing account increases = asset increase, but we classify CREDITS only, so this is on the debit side. (2) The deposit creates an obligation to perform services = unearned revenue/deferred revenue, which is a liability increase. Therefore, the credit side has one line: liability increase for the unearned revenue.
